name: Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyya
title: Wise Brother
shortDescription: Imam Hussain's (AS) half-brother who stayed in Medina and to whom the Imam entrusted the historic will of his uprising.
fullStory:
Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyya was the brave and wise son of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and the half-brother of Imam Hasan and Imam Hussain (AS). He was the standard-bearer of his father's army in the battles of Jamal and Siffin.

When Imam Hussain (AS) intended to leave Medina, Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyya could not join the caravan due to illness (or by the Imam's own order to preserve the Ahl al-Bayt's base in Medina). He tried to save his brother's life by offering compassionate advice and suggested the Imam go to Mecca or Yemen.

Before leaving Medina, Imam Hussain (AS) wrote his historic will and entrusted it to Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyya. In this will, it is stated: 'I have not risen to spread evil or to show off, but I only desire to enjoin the good and forbid the evil and reform the Ummah of my grandfather.'

After the event of Karbala, Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyya mourned his brother and later supported Mukhtar al-Thaqafi's uprising to avenge the martyrs of Karbala.
quoteArabic: إِنِّي لَمْ أَخْرُجْ أَشِراً وَلا بَطِراً... وَإِنَّما خَرَجْتُ لِطَلَبِ الإِصْلاحِ فِي أُمَّةِ جَدِّي...
quote: (The Imam's will to him:) I have not risen to spread evil or to show off, but I only desire to reform the Ummah of my grandfather.
